title: dicerandra frutescens var. cornutissima
intro: Dicerandra frutescens var. cornutissima, also known as the "Mountain Mint" or "Cornflower Balm", is a plant species belonging to the family Acanthaceae. It is a perennial herb native to the southeastern United States, valued for its medicinal and aromatic properties. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, offering benefits such as pain relief, reduced inflammation, and improved digestion.
